title: Visitor generates a random quote
summary: Visitor lands on /, picks a category (or 'all'), and clicks Generate. The page fetches /api/random-quote, which counts matching rows and picks one at a random offset. The previous quote id is sent as exclude so you never see the same quote twice in a row.

steps.label: findMany({ distinct: ['category'] })
steps.note: Server Component fetches the category list for the pill bar.

steps.label: SELECT DISTINCT category
steps.note: Query goes out over the Neon WebSocket adapter.

steps.label: categories[]
steps.note: Mapped to a flat string array.

steps.label: <QuoteGenerator categories={...}>
steps.note: Server-rendered HTML hydrates into a client component in the browser.

steps.label: click Generate
steps.note: User picks a category pill (or stays on 'all') and clicks the button.

steps.label: GET /api/random-quote
steps.note: Sends the active category and the previous quote id so it isn't picked again.

steps.label: quote.count(where)
steps.note: Counts matching rows to compute a random offset.

steps.label: quote.findMany({ skip, take: 1 })
steps.note: Cheap random pick for small libraries — fine here.

steps.label: SELECT … OFFSET ? LIMIT 1
steps.note: Two trips: one for count, one for the random row.

steps.label: JSON quote
steps.note: Returns null if no matches — the UI shows an empty-state.

steps.label: setQuote + bump key
steps.note: Incrementing quoteKey re-mounts the article so the quote-appear animation re-runs.

steps.label: fade in
steps.note: CSS quote-appear keyframes render the new quote.
